_description: Freespan and VIV assessment scenario parameters for GTM demo 1. Defines environmental conditions, span ranges, and soil properties for parametric screening of pipeline and jumper freespans.
_references: DNV-RP-C205 (2010) - Environmental Conditions and Environmental Loads

environment.current_direction_description: Perpendicular to pipe axis (worst case for VIV)
environment.current_profile: Near-bottom, assumed uniform over pipe diameter
environment._note: Velocities represent 100-year near-bottom current speeds at various locations. Range covers benign (GoM deepwater) to severe (North Sea, current-dominated straits).

span_lengths._note: Pipeline spans assessed per DNV-RP-F105. Jumper spans are unsupported lengths between bends or supports.

seabed_gap_ratios.definition: e/D where e = gap between pipe bottom and seabed, D = pipe outer diameter
seabed_gap_ratios._note: e/D = 0 means pipe resting on seabed at span shoulders. Higher e/D indicates erosion or irregular seabed. Stability parameter per DNV-RP-F105 Sec 4.2.

soft_clay.description: Soft marine clay typical of GoM, West Africa
soft_clay._note: Low stiffness leads to lower natural frequency and potentially shorter allowable spans

stiff_clay.description: Stiff overconsolidated clay typical of North Sea, Mediterranean
stiff_clay._note: Moderate stiffness. Most common assessment scenario.

sand._note: High stiffness increases natural frequency, generally allows longer spans

crossflow_viv._note: V_R = U / (f_n * D). Onset at V_R ~ 3.0 per DNV-RP-F105 Sec 4.3. Lock-in range 4-8.

inline_viv._note: Inline VIV onset at lower V_R. Often less critical for fatigue than crossflow.

stability_parameter._note: m_e = effective mass per unit length, zeta_T = total damping ratio (structural + soil + hydrodynamic). Higher K_s suppresses VIV amplitude.

_note: Full parametric sweep: 5 currents x 8 span lengths x 4 gap ratios x 3 soil types x 3 pipe sizes = 1440 pipeline cases; 5 x 8 x 4 x 3 x 5 = 2400 jumper cases.
